revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-4.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.notojima.org/highlight/2950.html Notojima Information] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:History of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Nanao, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Square Kofun]]

The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-4.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.notojima.org/highlight/2950.html Notojima Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-4.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.notojima.org/highlight/2950.html Notojima Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (burial mound) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other burial mounds have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-4.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.notojima.org/highlight/2950.html Notojima Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
