{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-4.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.notojima.org/highlight/2950.html Notojima Information] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji ware]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-4.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.notojima.org/highlight/2950.html Notojima Information] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]

Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|06|28|N|136|58|00|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-4.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.notojima.org/highlight/2950.html Notojima Information] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[Korean Peninsula]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-4.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.notojima.org/highlight/2950.html Notojima Information] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Susoezoana Kofun]][[en:Susoezoana Kofun]][[ja:須曽蝦夷穴古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11664581]] Wikidata name: Susoezoana Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.107667|N|136.966778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nanao|WD=Q695892}} | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[:en:corbel arch|corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[:en:Korea|Korea]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Nanao Line|Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[:en:corbel arch|corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[:en:Korea|Korea]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Nanao Line|Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Susoezoana Kofun]][[en:Susoezoana Kofun]][[ja:須曽蝦夷穴古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11664581]] Wikidata name: Susoezoana Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.107667|N|136.966778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nanao|WD=Q695892}} | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[:en:corbel arch|corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[:en:Korea|Korea]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Nanao Line|Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-4.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.notojima.org/highlight/2950.html Notojima Information] {{in lang|ja}}

The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[:en:corbel arch|corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[:en:Korea|Korea]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Nanao Line|Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Susoezoana Kofun]][[en:Susoezoana Kofun]][[ja:須曽蝦夷穴古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Susoezoana Kofun]][[en:Susoezoana Kofun]][[ja:須曽蝦夷穴古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Susoezoana Kofun]][[en:Susoezoana Kofun]][[ja:須曽蝦夷穴古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11664581]] Wikidata name: Susoezoana Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.107667|N|136.966778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nanao|WD=Q695892}} | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao, Ishikawa]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a [[:en:corbel arch|corbel arch]] construction. This is an extremely rare example of a [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]-style tumulus normally found in the northern [[:en:Korea|Korea]]. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] lid, fragments of iron straight swords, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Nanao Line|Nanao Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Susoezoana Kofun]][[en:Susoezoana Kofun]][[ja:須曽蝦夷穴古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Susoezoana Kofun]][[en:Susoezoana Kofun]][[ja:須曽蝦夷穴古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Susoezoana Kofun]][[en:Susoezoana Kofun]][[ja:須曽蝦夷穴古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11664581]] Wikidata name: Susoezoana Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.107667|N|136.966778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nanao|WD=Q695892}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao, Ishikawa}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}} slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a {{ill|corbel arch|en|corbel arch|WD=Q12550|lt=corbel arch}} construction. This is an extremely rare example of a {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}-style tumulus normally found in the northern {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} lid, fragments of iron straight swords, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]] on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nanao Line|en|Nanao Line|WD=Q5357471|lt=Nanao Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|WD=Q6573264|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]
==External links== {{commons category-inline}} *[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/siseki/1-4.html Ishikawa Prefectural information home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.notojima.org/highlight/2950.html Notojima Information] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Susoezoana Kofun]][[en:Susoezoana Kofun]][[ja:須曽蝦夷穴古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Susoezoana Kofun]][[en:Susoezoana Kofun]][[ja:須曽蝦夷穴古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Susoezoana Kofun]][[en:Susoezoana Kofun]][[ja:須曽蝦夷穴古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11664581]] Wikidata name: Susoezoana Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.107667|N|136.966778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nanao|WD=Q695892}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao, Ishikawa}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}} slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a {{ill|corbel arch|en|corbel arch|WD=Q12550|lt=corbel arch}} construction. This is an extremely rare example of a {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}-style tumulus normally found in the northern {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}}. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} lid, fragments of iron straight swords, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]] on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nanao Line|en|Nanao Line|WD=Q5357471|lt=Nanao Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|WD=Q6573264|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Susoezoana Kofun | native_name = 須曽蝦夷穴古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Ezosusoanakohun.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ishikawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Susoezoana Kofun | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.107667|N|136.966778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nanao|WD=Q695892|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park, museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Susoezoana Kofun'''|須曽蝦夷穴古墳|Susoezoana Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' (Kofun) of the middle [[Kofun period]] in the Suso neighbourhood of the city of {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao, Ishikawa|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201556 |title= 須曽蝦夷穴古墳|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Susoezoana Kofun is located on a hill on [[Notojima]], an island within Nanao Bay, at an elevation of approximately 80 meters above sea level. The tumulus is approximately 25 meters square and 4.5 meters tall and is aligned north-to-south. It is unusual in that it contains two lateral hole [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. One chamber is "T-shape" and the other is an inverted "L-shape" and open facing the ocean. Both have a length of roughly seven meters and are made from {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} slabs quarried on Noto island, although the "L-shape" chamber has partially collapsed. The construction method utilizes these stone slabs as bricks, and the ceiling has a {{ill|corbel arch|en|corbel arch|WD=Q12550|lt=corbel arch|1=Corbel arch}} construction. This is an extremely rare example of a {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}-style tumulus normally found in the northern {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}}. Both burial chambers have been open since antiquity, and their contents is unknown, but the tumulus of believed to date from the middle of the 7th century, after ''kofun'' ceased to be built in other locations of Japan. No other Kofuns have been found in the vicinity of this tumulus. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Although open since antiquity, during an excavation from 1953, fragments of a stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} lid, fragments of iron straight swords, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, and other earthenware shards were found in the ground. The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]] on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nanao Line|en|Nanao Line|WD=Q5357471|lt=Nanao Line|1=Nanao Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|WD=Q6573264|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ishikawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}} [[File:Susoezoanakofun corner.jpg|thumb|right|interior of Susoezoana Kofun]]

The adjacent {{nihongo|'''Ezoana History Center'''|蝦夷穴歴史センター|}} exhibits excavated items, photographs of the excavation, and artifacts from other archaeological sites on the island. The tumulus is located about 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]] on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nanao Line|en|Nanao Line|WD=Q5357471|lt=Nanao Line|1=Nanao Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
